sections.paragraph:
fut. apoc. יֵרַע ( Job 20:26).-

(1) to feed a flock, to pasture, to tend. (Arabic رعى id., and figuratively to guard, to care for, to rule. As to the origin I suspect it to be of the same stock as the verbs רָצָה, רָעָה, רָאָה and properly to have the sense of looking upon; whence רָצָה and רָעָה No. 3, to look upon with pleasure, gern fehen, רָעָה No. 1. to pasture a flock; prop. to look after, רָאָה to behold, to see.) Const. absol. Gen 37:13 Num 14:33 Son 1:7 followed by an acc. Gen 30:31 followed by בְּ (prop. to look upon), Gen 37:2 1Sa 16:11 17:34 . Part. רֹעֶה subst. a shepherd, Gen 13:7 26:20 fem. רֹעָה Gen 29:9. Figuratively to pasture, is used-(a) for to govern, to rule, of a prince (compare Hom. ποιμὴν λαῶν), 2Sa 5:2 7:7 Jer 23:2, seqq.; followed by בְּ Psa 78:71 of God, Psa 23:1, “Jehovah is my shepherd, I shall not want;” 28:9 80:2 of a teacher of virtue, Pro 10:21, שִׂפְתֵי צַדִּיק יִרְעוּ רַבִּים “the lips of the righteous feed many,” i.e. lead to virtue. So part. רֹעֶה a shepherd, metaph. used of God, Psa 23:1 of kings and princes, Jer 2:8 3:15 Zec 10:2 of a teacher of virtue and wisdom, Ecc 12:11.-(b) to nourish, to feed. Hos 9:2, “the floor and wine-press shall not feed them.”

(2) to feed (intrans.) as a flock, Isa 5:17 11:7 65:25 . Followed by an acc. of place, in which a flock feeds, Jer 50:19 Eze 34:14, 18 Eze 34:18, 19 Eze 34:19 Mic 7:14. Figuratively, to depasture, to consume. Mic 5:5, “they shall eat up (waste) the land of Assyria with the sword.” Job 20:26, יֵרַע שָׂרִיד בְּאָהֳלֹו “(the fire) devours what is left in his tent.” Jer 22:22 2:16 , יִרְעוּךְ קָדְקֹד “they devour the crown of the head.” Job 24:21, רֹועֶה עֲקָרָה “who oppressed the barren woman” (Chald. confringens).

(3) to delight in any person or thing (compare Lat. pasci aliqua re, i.e. to delight in). Chald. רְעָה id.; compare Hebr. רָצָה; Arab. رضا. Constr.-(a) followed by an acc. of pers. to delight in any one, to be his companion, Pro 13:20 28:7 29:3 .-(b) followed by an acc. of thing, Pro 15:14, “the mouth of fools יִרְעֶה אִוֶּלֶת delights in folly,” follows after folly. Psa 37:3, רְעֵה אֱמוּנָה “seek after truth.” רָעָה רוּחַ to seek after the wind, i.e. folly, Hos 12:2 compare Isa 44:20. Compare רְעוּת and רַעְיֹון רוּחַ.

Piel רֵעָה i.q. Kal No. 3 (or rather denom. from רֵעֶה), to join oneself as a companion to any one, Jud. 14:20 .

Hiphil, i.q. Kal No. 1, Psa 78:72.

Hithpael, to hold intercourse; followed by אֵת with any one, Pro 22:24.

Derivatives, רֵעֶה, רֵעַ No. II. רְעִי, רֹעִי, רְעוּת, רַעְיָה, רַעְיֹון, מִרְעֶה, מֵרֵעַ, מַרְעִית, and the pr.n. רְעוּ, רְעוּאֵל, רֵעִי. [and perhaps רוּת.]
